generatedSentence: Our respectable protagonist, a former goalkeeper, found solace not in the roar of the crowd, but in the quiet study of an ancient magazine. He was acutely aware of the world's ignorance regarding sustainable innovation, dreaming of a future where prism technology could power pottery wheels and automate daily chores. He spent hours watching the city lights, a silent whisper of a grander fantasy he wished to ascend, like a ladder to the stars. His expansive city view from his workshop, filled with art made from reclaimed driftwood, looked neglected from the outside, a somewhat wonky exterior hiding the footprint of genius within. Despite the lure of vanity, his voracious appetite for knowledge was pure, often sharing a jocular tale with a kind old man from the eastern district. He wore a funky, self-designed badge, labeled with symbols of his monthly objectives, a complex instrument of his grand design. However, a darker force began to infiltrate the city’s heart, led by a baboon-faced demagogue who unfurled a menacing banner near the historic landmark. Their morally corrupt regime played political mahjong, setting objectives for their obedient followers: a demand for a chunky tribute, a monthly payment for "protection," promising false achievement. The air filled with flowery but empty rhetoric, an utterly cynical play for power, accompanied by insidious whispers of dissent suppression. From his high perch, our hero observed the desecrated landscape, understanding the need to vaccinate the city against this hegemon. He saw glimpses of a lost heaven in the people's eyes, remarking on the dangerous generalization that painted dissenters as enemies. He knew an enchanted city could be reborn, envisioning a triumphant, mayoral future if he could only improve the people's ideal circumstances. The conflict exploded, a veritable kaboom echoing through the streets, as the demagogue, a political tyrannosaur, unleashed a metaphorical flamethrower of propaganda. Our hero, summoning immense valiance, knew this was exactly the moment to act. He saw the corrupt dynasty crumbling, its members gorging on waffles and rich eggnog while the people suffered. He sent a coded letter to his network, urging them to live up to their ideals, calling for a secret klatch near the old hospital with its grotesque gargoyles. Using a specialized gadget that manipulated gravity, he disrupted their communication, causing their mahjongg game of power to topple. He insisted his allies intervene, refusing to ignore the escalating tyranny. He would draw the line here. The grizzly truth of their oppression was laid bare for the majority, securing the first rewards of their resistance. He also noted the subtle mandarin scent of the demagogue's secret lair. A light drizzle fell, cleansing the air after the storm. The city, once pregnant with despair, was no longer pathetic. Through collective success, the oppressive regime was overturned. The hero enjoyed a quiet supper, a nocturne of peace settling over the autumn city. His small unit of devoted followers, the new brides of justice, knew they had restored order. They had effectively ransacked the old power structure, helping people recognize the manipulative tactics they had once overlooked. Their devotion to the city was unwavering, despite the digital downvotes from lingering sympathizers. The old leader's assassin-like rhetoric, his unkindness, and brainless decrees were no more. No longer would they babysit a loveless system. A new velocity of progress was established, change spreading evenly throughout the reformed society.
panelSentences: Our respectable protagonist, a former goalkeeper, found solace not in the roar of the crowd, but in the quiet study of an ancient magazine. He was acutely aware of the world's ignorance regarding sustainable innovation, dreaming of a future where prism technology could power pottery wheels and automate daily chores. He spent hours watching the city lights, a silent whisper of a grander fantasy he wished to ascend, like a ladder to the stars. His expansive city view from his workshop, filled with art made from reclaimed driftwood, looked neglected from the outside, a somewhat wonky exterior hiding the footprint of genius within. Despite the lure of vanity, his voracious appetite for knowledge was pure, often sharing a jocular tale with a kind old man from the eastern district. He wore a funky, self-designed badge, labeled with symbols of his monthly objectives, a complex instrument of his grand design.

However, a darker force began to infiltrate the city’s heart, led by a baboon-faced demagogue who unfurled a menacing banner near the historic landmark. Their morally corrupt regime played political mahjong, setting objectives for their obedient followers: a demand for a chunky tribute, a monthly payment for "protection," promising false achievement. The air filled with flowery but empty rhetoric, an utterly cynical play for power, accompanied by insidious whispers of dissent suppression. From his high perch, our hero observed the desecrated landscape, understanding the need to vaccinate the city against this hegemon. He saw glimpses of a lost heaven in the people's eyes, remarking on the dangerous generalization that painted dissenters as enemies. He knew an enchanted city could be reborn, envisioning a triumphant, mayoral future if he could only improve the people's ideal circumstances.

The conflict exploded, a veritable kaboom echoing through the streets, as the demagogue, a political tyrannosaur, unleashed a metaphorical flamethrower of propaganda. Our hero, summoning immense valiance, knew this was exactly the moment to act. He saw the corrupt dynasty crumbling, its members gorging on waffles and rich eggnog while the people suffered. He sent a coded letter to his network, urging them to live up to their ideals, calling for a secret klatch near the old hospital with its grotesque gargoyles. Using a specialized gadget that manipulated gravity, he disrupted their communication, causing their mahjongg game of power to topple. He insisted his allies intervene, refusing to ignore the escalating tyranny. He would draw the line here. The grizzly truth of their oppression was laid bare for the majority, securing the first rewards of their resistance. He also noted the subtle mandarin scent of the demagogue's secret lair.

A light drizzle fell, cleansing the air after the storm. The city, once pregnant with despair, was no longer pathetic. Through collective success, the oppressive regime was overturned. The hero enjoyed a quiet supper, a nocturne of peace settling over the autumn city. His small unit of devoted followers, the new brides of justice, knew they had restored order. They had effectively ransacked the old power structure, helping people recognize the manipulative tactics they had once overlooked. Their devotion to the city was unwavering, despite the digital downvotes from lingering sympathizers. The old leader's assassin-like rhetoric, his unkindness, and brainless decrees were no more. No longer would they babysit a loveless system. A new velocity of progress was established, change spreading evenly throughout the reformed society.
rawPanelSentences: Our **respectable** protagonist, a former **goalkeeper**, found solace not in the roar of the crowd, but in the quiet study of an ancient **magazine**. He was acutely aware of the world's **ignorance** regarding sustainable **innovation**, dreaming of a future where **prism** technology could power **pottery** wheels and **automate** daily chores. He spent hours **watching** the city lights, a silent **whisper** of a grander **fantasy** he wished to ascend, like a **ladder** to the stars. His expansive city **view** from his workshop, filled with art made from reclaimed **driftwood**, looked **neglected** from the outside, a somewhat **wonky** exterior hiding the **footprint** of genius within. Despite the lure of **vanity**, his **voracious** appetite for knowledge was pure, often sharing a **jocular** tale with a **kind** old man from the **eastern** district. He wore a **funky**, self-designed **badge**, **labeled** with symbols of his **monthly** objectives, a complex **instrument** of his grand design.

However, a darker force began to **infiltrate** the city’s heart, led by a **baboon**-faced demagogue who unfurled a menacing **banner** near the historic **landmark**. Their **morally** corrupt regime played political **mahjong**, setting **objectives** for their **obedient** followers: a demand for a **chunky** tribute, a monthly **payment** for "protection," promising false **achievement**. The air filled with **flowery** but empty rhetoric, an **utterly** cynical play for power, accompanied by insidious **whispers** of dissent suppression. From his high perch, our hero observed the desecrated **landscape**, understanding the need to **vaccinate** the city against this **hegemon**. He saw glimpses of a lost **heaven** in the people's eyes, **remarking** on the dangerous **generalization** that painted dissenters as enemies. He knew an **enchanted** city could be reborn, envisioning a **triumphant**, **mayoral** future if he could only **improve** the people's **ideal** circumstances.

The conflict exploded, a veritable **kaboom** echoing through the streets, as the demagogue, a political **tyrannosaur**, unleashed a metaphorical **flamethrower** of propaganda. Our hero, summoning immense **valiance**, knew this was **exactly** the moment to act. He saw the corrupt **dynasty** crumbling, its members gorging on **waffles** and rich **eggnog** while the people suffered. He sent a coded **letter** to his network, urging them to **live** up to their ideals, calling for a secret **klatch** near the old **hospital** with its grotesque **gargoyles**. Using a specialized **gadget** that manipulated **gravity**, he disrupted their communication, causing their **mahjongg** game of power to topple. He **insisted** his allies **intervene**, refusing to **ignore** the escalating tyranny. He would **draw** the line here. The **grizzly** truth of their oppression was laid bare for the **majority**, securing the first **rewards** of their resistance. He also noted the subtle **mandarin** scent of the demagogue's secret lair.

A light **drizzle** fell, cleansing the air after the storm. The city, once **pregnant** with despair, was no longer **pathetic**. Through collective **success**, the oppressive regime was overturned. The hero enjoyed a quiet **supper**, a **nocturne** of peace settling over the **autumn** city. His small **unit** of devoted followers, the new **brides** of justice, **knew** they had restored order. They had effectively **ransack**ed the old power structure, helping people **recognize** the manipulative tactics they had once **overlooked**. Their **devotion** to the city was unwavering, despite the digital **downvote**s from lingering sympathizers. The old leader's **assassin**-like rhetoric, his **unkindness**, and **brainless** decrees were no more. No longer would they **babysit** a **loveless** system. A new **velocity** of progress was established, change spreading **evenly** throughout the reformed society.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, Retro-futuristic style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. The protagonist is a gaunt, middle-aged man with sharp eyes, wearing a vintage aviator jacket adorned with glowing circuit patterns and a singular, polished bronze gear badge. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist, sitting amidst strange reclaimed tech, gazing thoughtfully out of a large window at a sprawling, neon-lit cityscape below, wide shot, slightly from behind. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A charismatic, baboon-faced demagogue on a raised platform, unfurling a propaganda banner to a downtrodden crowd in a polluted plaza, medium shot, slightly low angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist, dodging debris, activating a glowing gravity-manipulating gadget in a chaotic, collapsing control room filled with ornate mahjongg tiles, dynamic close-up, dramatic lighting. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist standing on a rooftop, silhouetted against a serene, autumn-dusk cityscape under a light drizzle, a small group of allies looking up at him with hope, establishing shot, high angle. cel-shading, bold ink outlines.

definitions.definition: Something of value given in return for an act.
definitions.example: For catching the thief, you'll get a nice reward.

definitions.definition: A prize promised for a certain deed or catch
definitions.example: The rewards for bringing in badly wanted criminals are printed on 'dead or alive' posters

definitions.definition: The result of an action, whether good or bad.
definitions.example: Is this the reward I get for telling the truth: to be put in jail?

definitions.definition: To give a reward to or for.
definitions.example: Why are you rewarding that bad behaviour?

definitions.definition: To recompense.
definitions.example: Decorations are meant to reward the most meritous acts and services.

definitions.definition: To give (something) as a reward.
Deserving respect.
definitions.example: His accomplishments, morals, loyalty, and stature make him a respectable person.

definitions.definition: Decent; satisfactory.
definitions.example: Turn up to the interview wearing something respectable.  She plays a respectable game of chess.  He got a respectable B+ on his last exam.

definitions.definition: Moderately well-to-do.
A designated player that attempts to prevent the opposing team from scoring by protecting a goal.
A grizzly bear.
definitions.example: Shotgun blast kills charging grizzly!

definitions.definition: In hydraulic mining, a grating used to catch and throw out large stones from the sluices.
Grey-haired, greyish.
A beverage based on milk, eggs, sugar, and nutmeg; often made alcoholic with rum, brandy or whisky; popular at Christmas.
More than half (50%) of some group.
definitions.example: The majority agreed that the new proposal was the best.

definitions.definition: The difference between the winning vote and the rest of the votes.
definitions.example: The winner with 53% had a 6% majority over the loser with 47%.

definitions.definition: Legal adulthood.
definitions.example: By the time I reached my majority, I had already been around the world twice.

definitions.definition: The office held by a member of the armed forces in the rank of major.
definitions.example: On receiving the news of his promotion, Charles Snodgrass said he was delighted to be entering his majority.

definitions.definition: Ancestors; ancestry.
A high government bureaucrat of the Chinese Empire
A pedantic or elitist bureaucrat
A pedantic senior person of influence in academia or literary circles
A senior civil servant
Pertaining to or reminiscent of mandarins; deliberately superior or complex; esoteric, highbrow, obscurantist
A perching duck that has a red bill, large white crescent above the eye and reddish face, Aix galericulata.
A non-academic periodical publication, generally consisting of sheets of paper folded in half and stapled at the fold.
An ammunition storehouse.
A chamber in a firearm enabling multiple rounds of ammunition to be fed into the firearm.
A reservoir or supply chamber for a stove, battery, camera, typesetting machine, or other apparatus.
A country or district especially rich in natural products.
A city viewed as a marketing center.
A store, or shop, where goods are kept for sale.
The condition of being uninformed or uneducated. Lack of knowledge or information.
(in the plural) Sins committed through ignorance.
The act of innovating; the introduction of something new, in customs, rites, etc.
A change effected by innovating; a change in customs
definitions.example: The others, whose time had been more actively employed, began to shew symptoms of innovation,—"the good wine did its good office." The frost of etiquette, and pride of birth, began to give way before the genial blessings of this benign constellation, and the formal appellatives with which the three dignitaries had hitherto addressed each other, were now familiarly abbreviated into Tully, Bally, and Killie.

definitions.definition: Something new, and contrary to established customs, manners, or rites.
A newly formed shoot, or the annually produced addition to the stems of many mosses.
A polyhedron with parallel ends of the same size and shape, the other faces being parallelogram-shaped sides.
A transparent block in the shape of a prism (typically with triangular ends), used to split or reflect light.
A crystal in which the faces are parallel to the vertical axis.
Fired ceramic wares that contain clay when formed.
definitions.example: The shelves were lined with pottery of all shapes and sizes.

definitions.definition: A potter's shop or workshop, where pottery is made.
definitions.example: I visited the old potteries and saw the pots being made.

definitions.definition: The potter's craft or art: making vessels from clay.
To replace or enhance human labor with machines.
definitions.example: Many offices still need to automate.

definitions.definition: To look at, see, or view for a period of time.
definitions.example: I'm tired of watching TV.

definitions.definition: To observe over a period of time; to notice or pay attention.
definitions.example: Put a little baking soda in some vinegar and watch what happens.

definitions.definition: To mind, attend, or guard.
definitions.example: He has to watch the kids that afternoon.

definitions.definition: To be wary or cautious of.
definitions.example: You should watch that guy. He has a reputation for lying.

definitions.definition: To attend to dangers to or regarding.
definitions.example: Watch what you say.

definitions.definition: To remain awake with a sick or dying person; to maintain a vigil.
To be vigilant or on one's guard.
definitions.example: For some must watch, while some must sleep: So runs the world away.

definitions.definition: To act as a lookout.
(of a buoy) To serve the purpose of a watchman by floating properly in its place.
To be awake.
To be on the lookout for; to wait for expectantly.
The act of one who watches.
The act of speaking in a quiet voice, especially, without vibration of the vocal cords.
(usually in the plural) A rumor.
definitions.example: There are whispers of rebellion all around.

definitions.definition: A faint trace or hint (of something).
definitions.example: The soup had just a whisper of basil.

definitions.definition: A low rustling sound, like that of the wind in leaves.
A private message to an individual in a chat room.

To speak softly, or under the breath, so as to be heard only by one near at hand; to utter words without sonant breath; to talk without that vibration in the larynx which gives sonorous, or vocal, sound.
To mention privately and confidentially, or in a whisper.
To make a low, sibilant sound.
To speak with suspicion or timorous caution; to converse in whispers, as in secret plotting.
To address in a whisper, or low voice.
To prompt secretly or cautiously; to inform privately.
That which comes from one's imagination.
The literary genre generally dealing with themes of magic and the supernatural, imaginary worlds and creatures, etc.
A fantastical design.
The drug gamma-hydroxybutyric acid.
To fantasize (about).
To have a fancy for; to be pleased with; to like.
To imagine; to conceive mentally.
A frame, usually portable, of wood, metal, or rope, used for ascent and descent, consisting of two side pieces to which are fastened rungs (cross strips or rounds acting as steps).
A series of stages by which one progresses to a better position.
The hierarchy or ranking system within an organization, such as the corporate ladder.
A length of unravelled fabric in a knitted garment, especially in nylon stockings; a run.
In the game of go, a sequence of moves following a zigzag pattern and ultimately leading to the capture of the attacked stones.
To arrange or form into a shape of a ladder.
To ascend (a building, a wall, etc.) using a ladder.
Of a knitted garment: to develop a ladder as a result of a broken thread.
definitions.example: Oh damn it, I've laddered my tights!

definitions.definition: That which is vain, futile, or worthless; that which is of no value, use or profit.
Excessive pride in or admiration of one's own abilities, appearance or achievements.

A dressing table used to apply makeup, preen, and coif hair. The table is normally quite low and similar to a desk, with drawers and one or more mirrors on top. Either a chair or bench is used to sit upon.
A washbasin installed into a permanently fixed storage unit, used as an item of bathroom furniture.
Emptiness.
Any idea, theory or statement that is without foundation.
definitions.example: It is a vanity to say that if two stones are dropped from a tower, the heavier will experience the greater acceleration.

definitions.definition: Wanting or devouring great quantities of food.
Having a great appetite for anything (e.g., a voracious reader).
Humorous, amusing or joking.
definitions.example: All we had was a short and jocular conversation.

definitions.definition: The sound of an explosion, typically seen in comic strips or heard in a light-hearted conversation.
The sound of an explosion, typically seen in comic strips or heard in a light-hearted conversation.
A type, race or category; a group of entities that have common characteristics such that they may be grouped together.
definitions.example: This is a strange kind of tobacco.

definitions.definition: A makeshift or otherwise atypical specimen.
definitions.example: The opening served as a kind of window.

definitions.definition: One's inherent nature; character, natural disposition.
Family, lineage.
Manner.
Goods or services used as payment, as e.g. in barter.
Equivalent means used as response to an action.
definitions.example: I'll pay in kind for his insult.

definitions.definition: Each of the two elements of the communion service, bread and wine.
Of, facing, situated in, or related to the east.
(of a wind) Blowing from the east; easterly.
Oriental.
Any large bipedal carnivorous dinosaur, of the family Tyrannosauridae, that lived in North America during the Cretaceous period.
A floating piece, or pieces, of wood that drifts with the current.
Such a piece of wood that has been cast ashore.
To fail to care for or attend to something.
definitions.example: to neglect duty or business;  to neglect to pay debts

definitions.definition: To omit to notice; to forbear to treat with attention or respect; to slight.
definitions.example: to neglect strangers

definitions.definition: To fail to do or carry out something due to oversight or carelessness.
Suffering from neglect.
A subgenre of electronic music employing unstable rhythms, complex time signatures, and mid-range synths.
Lopsided, misaligned or off-centre.
Feeble, shaky or rickety.
(especially Usenet) Suffering from intermittent bugs.
Generally incorrect.
The impression of the foot in a soft substance such as sand or snow.
Space required by a piece of equipment.
definitions.example: This computer has a smaller footprint.

definitions.definition: The amount of hard drive space required for a program.
The audit trail left by a crashed program.
Profession or lifestyle.
definitions.example: He is following in his father's footprints.

definitions.definition: The surface space occupied by a structure.
definitions.example: the footprint of a building

definitions.definition: A company's geographic market presence.
definitions.example: The store, which is slated to open next month, increases the company's footprint in the market to 14 locations.

definitions.definition: The ecological impact of a human activity, machine, etc.
definitions.example: My carbon footprint is very high.

definitions.definition: Availability of a satellite from the ground.
A device that projects a flame for starting fires, and sometimes also additional fuel to help ignition. Used either as a weapon or a tool.
Offbeat, unconventional or eccentric.
Not quite right; of questionable quality; not appropriate to the context.
Cool; great; excellent.
Having or relating to the smell of funk.
Relating to or reminiscent of various genres of African American music, especially funk.
Relating to, or characterized by, great fear, or funking.
A distinctive mark, token, sign, emblem or cognizance, worn on one's clothing, as an insignia of some rank, or of the membership of an organization.
definitions.example: the badge of a society; the badge of a policeman

definitions.definition: A small nameplate, identifying the wearer, and often giving additional information.
A card, sometimes with a barcode or magnetic strip, granting access to a certain area.
Something characteristic; a mark; a token.
(thieves' cant) A brand on the hand of a thief, etc.
definitions.example: He has got his badge, and piked: He was burned in the hand, and is at liberty.

definitions.definition: A carved ornament on the stern of a vessel, containing a window or the representation of one.
A distinctive mark worn by servants, retainers, and followers of royalty or nobility, who, being beneath the rank of gentlemen, have no right to armorial bearings.
A small overlay on an icon that shows additional information about that item, such as the number of new alerts or messages.
An icon or emblem awarded to a user for some achievement.
definitions.example: When you have checked in to the site from ten different cities, you unlock the Traveller badge.

definitions.definition: To mark or distinguish with a badge.
definitions.example: The television was badged as 'GE', but wasn't made by them.

definitions.definition: To show a badge to.
definitions.example: He calmed down a lot when the policeman badged him.

definitions.definition: To enter a restricted area by showing one's badge.
To put a label (a ticket or sign) on (something).
definitions.example: The shop assistant labeled all the products in the shop.

definitions.definition: (ditransitive) To give a label to (someone or something) in order to categorise that person or thing.
definitions.example: He's been unfairly labeled as a cheat, although he's only ever cheated once.

definitions.definition: To replace specific atoms by their isotope in order to track the presence or movement of this isotope through a reaction, metabolic pathway or cell.
To add a detectable substance, either transiently or permanently, to a biological substance in order to track the presence of the label-substance combination either in situ or in vitro
Having a label, tagged.
definitions.example: The butterfly collection had each specimen labeled with the scientific name on a little piece of paper.

definitions.definition: Defined or described.
definitions.example: He was labeled as a racist for his otherwise innocent remark.

definitions.definition: Having an atom replaced by a radioactive isotope
definitions.example: Insulin labeled with iodine-125 was used as a tracer

definitions.definition: The quality of being valiant; heroism, bravery or valour.
(manner) without approximation; precisely.
definitions.example: He divided the coins exactly in half.

definitions.definition: (focus) Used to provide emphasis.
definitions.example: He did it that way exactly to prove the point.

definitions.definition: Signifies agreement or recognition.
definitions.example: So you're saying that we have only three days left? — Yes, exactly!

definitions.definition: A publication that is published once a month.
The menstrual period.
Occurring every month.
Every month.
Any undesirable substance or group of cells that has made its way into part of the body.
To surreptitiously penetrate, enter or gain access to.
(of a liquid) To pass through something by filtration.
To cause (a liquid) to pass through something by filtration.
To invade or penetrate a tissue or organ.
definitions.example: High-grade tumors often infiltrate surrounding structures.

definitions.definition: To send (soldiers, spies etc.) through gaps in the enemy line.
(of an intravenous needle) To move from a vein, remaining in the body.
An Old World monkey of the genus Papio, having dog-like muzzles and large canine teeth, cheek pouches, a short tail, and naked callosities on the buttocks.
A foolish or boorish person.
A flag or standard used by a military commander, monarch or nation.
(by extension) The military unit under such a flag or standard.
(by extension) A military or administrative subdivision.
Any large sign, especially when made of soft material or fabric.
definitions.example: The mayor hung a banner across Main Street to commemorate the town's 100th anniversary.

definitions.definition: A large piece of cloth with a slogan, motto, or emblem carried in a demonstration or other procession or suspended in some conspicuous place.
(by extension) A cause or purpose; a campaign or movement.
definitions.example: They usually make their case under the banner of environmentalism.

definitions.definition: The title of a newspaper as printed on its front page; the nameplate; masthead.
A type of advertisement on a web page or on television, usually taking the form of a graphic or animation above or alongside the content.
The principal standard of a knight.
A type of administrative division in Inner Mongolia, China (хошуу/旗) and Tuva (кожуун), made during the Qing dynasty. At this time, Outer Mongolia and part of Xinjiang were also divided this way.
To adorn with a banner.
To display as a banner headline.
Exceptional; very good.
An object that marks the boundary of a piece of land (usually a stone, or a tree).
A recognizable natural or man-made feature used for navigation.
A notable location with historical, cultural, or geographical significance.
A major event or discovery.
definitions.example: a landmark paper in neurosurgery

definitions.definition: To officially designate a site or building as a landmark.
In terms of morals or ethics.
definitions.example: Morally, it is a difficult issue to deal with.

definitions.definition: In keeping of requirements of morality.
definitions.example: to behave morally

definitions.definition: To all intents and purposes; practically.
A game (originally Chinese) for four players, using a collection of tiles divided into five or six suits.
A solitaire game using the same tiles, where the player wins by removing pairs of matching exposed tiles until none remain.
A device used to produce music.
definitions.example: The violinist was a master of her instrument.

definitions.definition: A means or agency for achieving an effect.
A measuring or displaying device.
definitions.example: The instrument detected an increase in radioactivity.

definitions.definition: A tool, implement used for manipulation or measurement.
definitions.example: The dentist set down his tray of instruments.

definitions.definition: A legal document, such as a contract, deed, trust, mortgage, power, indenture, or will.
definitions.example: A bond indenture is the instrument that gives a bond its value.

definitions.definition: A person used as a mere tool for achieving a goal.
To apply measuring devices.
To devise, conceive, cook up, plan.
To perform upon an instrument; to prepare for an instrument.
definitions.example: a sonata instrumented for orchestra

definitions.definition: A material object that physically exists.
A goal that is striven for.
(grammar) The objective case.
(grammar) a noun or pronoun in the objective case.
The lens or lenses of a camera, microscope, or other optical device closest to the object being examined.
One who obeys.
Willing to comply with the commands, orders, or instructions of those in authority.
definitions.example: Jessica was so intensely obedient of her parents that her brother sometimes thought she was a robot.

definitions.definition: Having chunks.
definitions.example: I ate a chunky bar of chocolate.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Fat.
definitions.example: I prefer chunky blonde girls to skinny brunettes.

definitions.definition: Of a cat: having a large, solid bodyline.
definitions.example: The British Shorthair has a distinctively chunky body, dense coat and broad face.

definitions.definition: The act of paying.
A sum of money paid in exchange for goods or services.
The act of achieving or performing; a successful performance; accomplishment
A great or heroic deed or feat; something accomplished by valor or boldness

An award for completing a particular task or meeting an objective in a video game.
definitions.example: Finishing the game does not give you a 100% score until you have unlocked all of the achievements.

(usually in the plural) A rumor.

definitions.definition: A low rustling sound, like that of the wind in leaves.
A private message to an individual in a chat room.

To speak softly, or under the breath, so as to be heard only by one near at hand; to utter words without sonant breath; to talk without that vibration in the larynx which gives sonorous, or vocal, sound.
To mention privately and confidentially, or in a whisper.
To make a low, sibilant sound.
To speak with suspicion or timorous caution; to converse in whispers, as in secret plotting.
To address in a whisper, or low voice.
To prompt secretly or cautiously; to inform privately.
Pertaining to flowers.
Decorated with or abundant in flowers.
(of a speech or piece of writing) overly complicated or elaborate; with grandiloquent expressions
Completely, entirely, to the fullest extent
definitions.example: I have failed you utterly.

definitions.definition: An Italian sauce made of ground meat and tomato.
A dish served with bolognese.
A portion of land or territory which the eye can comprehend in a single view, including all the objects it contains.
A sociological aspect of a physical area.
A picture representing a real or imaginary scene by land or sea, the main subject being the general aspect of nature, as fields, hills, forests, water, etc.
The pictorial aspect of a country.
A mode of printing where the horizontal sides are longer than the vertical sides
A space, indoor or outdoor and natural or man-made (as in "designed landscape")
A situation that is presented, a scenario
definitions.example: The software patent landscape has changed considerably in the last years

definitions.definition: To create or maintain a landscape.
Treat with a vaccine to produce immunity against a disease.
A dominating leader or force, especially that which dominates a separate political entity; a hegemonist.
The sky, specifically:
The abode of God or the gods, traditionally conceived as beyond the sky; especially:
The afterlife of the blessed dead, traditionally conceived as opposed to an afterlife of the wicked and unjust (compare hell); specifically:
(by extension) Any paradise; any blissful place or experience.
(by extension) A state of bliss; a peaceful ecstasy.
(with a modifier) Similarly blissful afterlives, places, or states for particular people, animals, or objects.
To transport to the abode of God, the gods, or the blessed.
To beatify, enchant, or please greatly.
To beautify, to make into a paradise.
To mark again.
To make a remark or remarks; to comment.
To express in words or writing; to state; to make a comment
definitions.example: He remarked that it was getting late.

definitions.definition: To pay heed to; notice; to take notice of
To mark in a notable manner; to distinguish clearly; to make noticeable or conspicuous; to point out.
The formulation of general concepts from specific instances by abstracting common properties.
Inductive reasoning from detailed facts to general principles.
An oversimplified or exaggerated conception, opinion, or image of the members of a group.
A proof, axiom, problem, or definition which includes another's cases, and also some additional cases.
definitions.example: A hypersphere is a generalization of a sphere across more than three dimensions.

definitions.definition: To attract and delight, to charm.
To cast a spell upon (often one that attracts or charms).
To magically enhance or degrade an item.
Charmed, delighted, enraptured.
Under the influence of enchantment.
A greeting used when introduced to someone for the first time, especially by a man when introduced to a woman.
Celebrating victory.
definitions.example: Athena, war's triumphant maid...

definitions.definition: Relating to a mayor (or the office of the mayor).
definitions.example: The mayoral seal was applied to all official correspondence of the mayor.

definitions.definition: To make (something) better; to increase the value or productivity (of something).
definitions.example: Buying more servers would improve performance.

definitions.definition: To become better.
definitions.example: I have improved since taking the tablets.

definitions.definition: To disprove or make void; to refute.
To disapprove of; to find fault with; to reprove; to censure.
definitions.example: to improve negligence

definitions.definition: To use or employ to good purpose; to turn to profitable account.
definitions.example: to improve one's time;  to improve his means

definitions.definition: A perfect standard of beauty, intellect etc., or a standard of excellence to aim at.
A subring closed under multiplication by its containing ring.
(lattice theory) A non-empty lower set (of a partially ordered set) which is closed under binary suprema (a.k.a. joins).
A collection of sets, considered small or negligible, such that every subset of each member and the union of any two members are also members of the collection.
(Lie theory) A Lie subalgebra (subspace that is closed under the Lie bracket) 𝖍 of a given Lie algebra 𝖌 such that the Lie bracket [𝖌,𝖍] is a subset of 𝖍.
Optimal; being the best possibility.
Perfect, flawless, having no defects.
Pertaining to ideas, or to a given idea.
Existing only in the mind; conceptual, imaginary.
Teaching or relating to the doctrine of idealism.
definitions.example: the ideal theory or philosophy

definitions.definition: Not actually present, but considered as present when limits at infinity are included.
definitions.example: An ideal triangle in the hyperbolic disk is one bounded by three geodesics that meet precisely on the circle.

definitions.definition: A series of rulers or dynasts from one family.
A team or organization which has an extended period of success or dominant performance.
A flat pastry pressed with a grid pattern.
definitions.example: The brunch was waffles with strawberries and whipped cream.

definitions.definition: A potato waffle, a savoury flat potato cake with the same kind of grid pattern.
A large kerchief, usually colourful and used either as headgear or as a handkerchief, neckerchief, bikini, or sweatband.

A style of calico printing, in which white or bright spots are produced upon cloth previously dyed a uniform red or dark colour, by discharging portions of the color by chemical means, while the rest of the cloth is under pressure.
A symbol in an alphabet.
definitions.example: There are twenty-six letters in the English alphabet.

definitions.definition: A written or printed communication, generally longer and more formal than a note.
definitions.example: I wrote a letter to my sister about my life.

definitions.definition: The literal meaning of something, as distinguished from its intended and remoter meaning (often contrasted with the spirit).
(plural) Literature.
definitions.example: Benjamin Franklin was multiskilled – a scientist, politician and a man of letters.

definitions.definition: A division unit of a piece of law marked by a letter of the alphabet.
A size of paper, 8½ in × 11 in (215.9 mm × 279.4 mm, US paper sizes rounded to the nearest 5 mm).
A size of paper, 215 mm × 280 mm.
A single type; type, collectively; a style of type.
To print, inscribe, or paint letters on something.
(scholastic) To earn a varsity letter (award).

An identifying initial of the academic institution that is awarded to a member of a varsity team. It is made from structured cloth, and intended to be sewn to a piece of clothing such as a varsity sweater or varsity jacket.
To be alive; to have life.
definitions.example: He's not expected to live for more than a few months.

definitions.definition: To have permanent residence somewhere, to inhabit, to reside.
definitions.example: I live at 2a Acacia Avenue.  He lives in LA, but he's staying here over the summer.

definitions.definition: To survive; to persevere; to continue.
definitions.example: Her memory lives in that song.

definitions.definition: To cope.
definitions.example: You'll just have to live with it!  I can't live in a world without you.

definitions.definition: To pass life in a specified manner.
definitions.example: It is difficult to live in poverty.   And they lived happily ever after.

definitions.definition: To spend, as one's life; to pass; to maintain; to continue in, constantly or habitually.
definitions.example: To live an idle or a useful life.

definitions.definition: To act habitually in conformity with; to practice; to exemplify in one's way of life.
To outlast danger; to float (said of a ship, boat, etc).
definitions.example: No ship could live in such a storm.

definitions.definition: (followed by "on" or "upon") To maintain or support one's existence; to provide for oneself; to feed; to subsist.
definitions.example: It is hard to live on the minimum wage.   They lived on stale bread.

definitions.definition: To make the most of life; to experience a full, rich life.
definitions.example: I'm sick of spending every day studying at home: I want to go out there and live!

definitions.definition: (physical) Visual perception.
A picture, drawn or painted; a sketch.
definitions.example: a fine view of Lake George

definitions.definition: (psychological) Opinion, judgement, imagination.
A virtual or logical table composed of the result set of a query in relational databases.
The part of a computer program which is visible to the user and can be interacted with
A wake.
To look at.
definitions.example: The video was viewed by millions of people.

definitions.definition: To regard in a stated way.
definitions.example: I view it as a serious breach of trust.

definitions.definition: An informal social gathering, especially one held over coffee for the purpose of conversation.
A large medical facility, usually in a building with multiple floors, where seriously ill or injured patients are given extensive medical and/or surgical treatment.
A building founded for the long-term care of its residents, such as an almshouse. The residents may have no physical ailments, but simply need financial support.
A place of lodging.
Hospitable.
A carved grotesque figure on a spout which conveys water away from the gutters.
Any decorative carved grotesque figure on a building.
A fictional winged monster.
An ugly woman.
A thing whose name cannot be remembered; thingamajig, doohickey.
Any device or machine, especially one whose name cannot be recalled. Often either clever or complicated.
definitions.example: He bought a neat new gadget for shredding potatoes.

definitions.definition: Any consumer electronics product.
A sequence of machine code instructions crafted as part of an exploit that attempts to divert execution to a memory location chosen by the attacker.
The state or condition of having weight; weight; heaviness.
The state or condition of being grave; seriousness.
definitions.example: I hope you appreciate the gravity of the situation.

definitions.definition: The lowness of a note.
Force on Earth's surface, of the attraction by the Earth's masses, and the centrifugal pseudo-force caused by the Earth's rotation, resulting from gravitation.
(in casual discussion, also) Gravitation, universal force exercised by two bodies onto each other (gravity and gravitation are often used interchangeably).
Specific gravity.
A game (originally Chinese) for four players, using a collection of tiles divided into five or six suits.
A solitaire game using the same tiles, where the player wins by removing pairs of matching exposed tiles until none remain.
(with on or upon or (that + ordinary verb form)) To hold up a claim emphatically.
definitions.example: I insist that my secretary dresses nicely.

definitions.definition: (sometimes with on or upon or (that + subjunctive)) To demand continually that something happen or be done.
definitions.example: I insist that my secretary dress nicely.

definitions.definition: To stand (on); to rest (upon); to lean (upon).
To become involved in a situation, so as to alter or prevent an action.
definitions.example: The police had to be called to intervene in the fight.

definitions.definition: To occur, fall, or come between, points of time, or events.
definitions.example: An instant intervened between the flash and the report.

definitions.definition: To occur or act as an obstacle or delay.
definitions.example: Nothing intervened to prevent the undertaking.

definitions.definition: To say (something) in the middle of a conversation or discussion between other people, or to respond to a situation involving other people.
To come between, or to be between, persons or things.
definitions.example: The Mediterranean intervenes between Europe and Africa.

definitions.definition: In a suit to which one has not been made a party, to put forward a defense of one's interest in the subject matter.
definitions.example: an application for leave (i.e. permission) to intervene

definitions.definition: To deliberately not listen or pay attention to.
definitions.example: A problem ignored is a problem doubled.

definitions.definition: To pretend to not notice someone or something.
Fail to notice.
Not to know.
The result of a contest that neither side has won; a tie.
definitions.example: The game ended in a draw.

definitions.definition: The procedure by which the result of a lottery is determined.
definitions.example: The draw is on Saturday.

definitions.definition: Something that attracts e.g. a crowd.
The result of a two-innings match in which at least one side did not complete all their innings before time ran out (as distinguished from a tie).
A golf shot that (for the right-handed player) curves intentionally to the left. See hook, slice, fade.
A shot that is intended to land gently in the house (the circular target) without knocking out other stones; cf. takeout.
A dry stream bed that drains surface water only during periods of heavy rain or flooding.
A bag of cannabis.
Cannabis.
In a commission-based job, an advance on future (potential) commissions given to an employee by the employer.
A situation in which one or more players has four cards of the same suit or four out of five necessary cards for a straight and requires a further card to make their flush or straight.
The act of pulling back the strings in preparation of firing.
The spin or twist imparted to a ball etc. by a drawing stroke.
To move or develop something.
To exert or experience force.
(fluidic) To remove or separate or displace.
To change in size or shape.
To attract or be attracted.
(usually as draw on or draw upon) To rely on; utilize as a source.
definitions.example: She had to draw upon her experience to solve the problem.

definitions.definition: To disembowel.
To end a game in a draw (with neither side winning).
definitions.example: We drew last time we played.  I drew him last time I played him.  I drew my last game against him.

definitions.definition: To choose by means of a random selection process.
To make a shot that lands gently in the house (the circular target) without knocking out other stones.
To play (a short-length ball directed at the leg stump) with an inclined bat so as to deflect the ball between the legs and the wicket.
To hit (the ball) with the toe of the club so that it is deflected toward the left.
To strike (the cue ball) below the center so as to give it a backward rotation which causes it to take a backward direction on striking another ball.
Light rain.
Very small, numerous, and uniformly dispersed water drops, mist, or sprinkle. Unlike fog droplets, drizzle falls to the ground.
definitions.example: No longer pouring, the rain outside slowed down to a faint drizzle.

definitions.definition: Water.
definitions.example: Stop drinking all of my drizzle!

definitions.definition: A cake onto which icing, honey or syrup has been drizzled in an artistic manner.
To rain lightly.
definitions.example: We had planned a picnic for Joe's birthday, but it ended up drizzling all day.

definitions.definition: To shed slowly in minute drops or particles.
To pour slowly and evenly, especially oil or honey in cooking.
definitions.example: The recipe says to toss the salad and then drizzle olive oil on it.

definitions.definition: To cover by pouring in this manner.
definitions.example: The recipe says to toss the salad and then drizzle it in olive oil.

definitions.definition: To urinate.
To carry out parfilage, the process of unravelling.
A pregnant person.
Carrying developing offspring within the body.
definitions.example: I went to the doctor and, guess what, I'm pregnant!

definitions.definition: Having numerous possibilities or implications; full of promise; abounding in ability, resources, etc.
definitions.example: a pregnant pause

definitions.definition: Fertile, prolific (usually of soil, ground etc.).
Affording entrance; receptive; yielding; willing; open; prompt.
Ready-witted; clever; ingenious.
Arousing pity, sympathy, or compassion.
definitions.example: The child’s pathetic pleas for forgiveness stirred the young man’s heart.

definitions.definition: Arousing scornful pity or contempt, often due to miserable inadequacy.
definitions.example: You can't even run two miles? That’s pathetic.

definitions.definition: Expressing or showing anger; passionate.
Trochlear.
The achievement of one's aim or goal.
definitions.example: His third attempt to pass the entrance exam was a success.

definitions.definition: Financial profitability.
definitions.example: Don't let success go to your head.

definitions.definition: One who, or that which, achieves assumed goals.
definitions.example: Scholastically, he was a success.

definitions.definition: The fact of getting or achieving wealth, respect or fame.
definitions.example: She is country music's most recent success.

definitions.definition: Something which happens as a consequence; the outcome or result.
Food consumed before going to bed.
Any meal eaten in the evening; dinner eaten in the evening, rather than at noon.
A meal from a chip shop consisting of a deep-fried food with chips.
definitions.example: a fish supper; a pizza supper

definitions.definition: To consume a snack before retiring.
To eat dinner (see above).
A work of art relating or dedicated to the night.
A dreamlike or pensive composition, usually for the piano.

Traditionally the third of the four seasons, when deciduous trees lose their leaves; typically regarded as being from September 24 to December 22 in parts of the Northern Hemisphere, and the months of March, April and May in the Southern Hemisphere.
(by extension) The time period when someone or something is past its prime.
A person with relatively dark hair and a warm skin tone, seen as best suited to certain colours in clothing.
Of or relating to autumn; autumnal
definitions.example: autumn leaves

definitions.definition: A particular, minute unit of mass, defined differently for different substances, but so that varying substances of the same general type have the property that one international unit of the one has the same effect on the human body as one international unit of the other.
Oneness, singularity, seen as a component of a whole number; a magnitude of one.
A standard measure of a quantity.
definitions.example: The centimetre is a unit of length.

definitions.definition: The number one.
An organized group comprising people and/or equipment.
definitions.example: He was a member of a special police unit.

definitions.definition: A member of a military organization.
definitions.example: The fifth tank brigade moved in with 20 units. (i.e., 20 tanks)

definitions.definition: Any military element whose structure is prescribed by competent authority, such as a table of organization and equipment; specifically, part of an organization.
An organization title of a subdivision of a group in a task force.
A standard or basic quantity into which an item of supply is divided, issued or detailed. In this meaning, also called unit of issue.

With regard to Reserve Components of the Armed Forces, denotes a Selected Reserve unit organized, equipped, and trained for mobilization to serve on active duty as a unit or to augment or be augmented by another unit. Headquarters and support functions without wartime missions are not considered units.
The identity element, neutral element.
An element having an inverse, an invertible element; an associate of the unity.
In an adjunction, a natural transformation from the identity functor of the domain of the left adjoint functor to the composition of the right adjoint functor with the left adjoint functor.

A volume of rock or ice of identifiable origin and age range that is defined by the distinctive and dominant, easily mapped and recognizable petrographic, lithologic or paleontologic features (facies) that characterize it.
An item which may be sold singly.
definitions.example: We shipped nearly twice as many units this month as last month.

definitions.definition: A unit of alcohol.
One kilowatt-hour (as recorded on an electricity meter).

A measure of housing equivalent to the living quarters of one household; an apartment where a group of apartments is contained in one or more multi-storied buildings or a group of dwellings is in one or more single storey buildings, usually arranged around a driveway.
A gold coin of the reign of James I, worth twenty shillings.
A work unit.
A physically large person.
For each unit.
definitions.example: We have to keep our unit costs down if we want to make a profit.

definitions.definition: Having a size or magnitude of one.
A woman in the context of her own wedding; one who is going to marry or has just been married.
An object ardently loved.
To make a bride of
An individual loop or other device connecting the patterns in lacework
To perceive the truth or factuality of; to be certain of or that.
definitions.example: He knew something terrible was going to happen.

definitions.definition: To be aware of; to be cognizant of.
definitions.example: Did you know Michelle and Jack were getting divorced? ― Yes, I knew.

definitions.definition: To be acquainted or familiar with; to have encountered.
definitions.example: I know your mother, but I’ve never met your father.

definitions.definition: To experience.
definitions.example: Their relationship knew ups and downs.

definitions.definition: To be able to distinguish, to discern, particularly by contrast or comparison; to recognize the nature of.
definitions.example: I wouldn't know one from the other.

definitions.definition: To recognize as the same (as someone or something previously encountered) after an absence or change.
To understand or have a grasp of through experience or study.
definitions.example: His mother tongue is Italian, but he also knows French and English.

definitions.definition: To have sexual relations with. This meaning normally specified in modern English as e.g. to ’know someone in the biblical sense’ or to ‘know Biblically.’
To have knowledge; to have information, be informed.
definitions.example: He knows about 19th century politics.

definitions.definition: To be or become aware or cognizant.
definitions.example: Did you know Michelle and Jack were getting divorced? ― Yes, I knew.

definitions.definition: To be acquainted (with another person).
To be able to play or perform (a song or other piece of music).
definitions.example: Do you know "Blueberry Hill"?

definitions.definition: Eager search.
To loot or pillage. See also sack.
To make a vigorous and thorough search of (a place, person) with a view to stealing something, especially when leaving behind a state of disarray.
definitions.example: to ransack a house for valuables

definitions.definition: To examine carefully; to investigate.
To violate; to ravish; to deflower.
To match (something or someone which one currently perceives) to a memory of some previous encounter with the same person or thing.
definitions.example: I recognised his face immediately.

definitions.definition: To acknowledge the existence or legality of; to treat as valid or worthy of consideration.
definitions.example: The US and a number of EU countries are expected to recognize Kosovo on Monday.

definitions.definition: (or with clause) To acknowledge or consider (as being a certain thing or having a certain quality or property).
definitions.example: I recognize that my behaviour has been unacceptable.

definitions.definition: To realize or discover the nature of something; apprehend quality in.
To show formal appreciation of, as with an award, commendation etc.
definitions.example: His services were recognized in a testimonial.

definitions.definition: To review; to examine again.
To reconnoiter.
To have the property to bind to specific antigens.
To offer a view (of something) from a higher position.
definitions.example: Our hotel room overlooks the lake.

definitions.definition: To fail to notice; to look over and beyond (anything) without seeing it.
definitions.example: These errors were overlooked by the proofreaders.

definitions.definition: To pretend not to have noticed (something, especially a mistake or flaw); to pass over (something) without censure or punishment.
definitions.example: I’m not willing to overlook such bad behaviour.

definitions.definition: To look down upon (something) from a place that is over or above.
definitions.example: to overlook a valley from a hill

definitions.definition: To supervise, oversee; to watch over.
definitions.example: to overlook a gang of laborers

definitions.definition: To observe or watch (someone or something) surreptitiously or secretly.
To inspect (something); to examine; to look over carefully or repeatedly.
To look upon with an evil eye; to bewitch by looking upon; to fascinate.
Missed, unnoticed.
The act or state of devoting or being devoted.
Feeling of strong or fervent affection; dedication
Religious veneration, zeal, or piety.
A prayer (often found in the plural)
(in the plural) Religious offerings; alms.
A vote against something that reduces a cumulative tally of popularity.
To vote against, reducing a cumulative tally of popularity.
A member of the Nizari Ismaili Muslim community of the Alamut Period
Someone who intentionally kills a person, especially a professional who kills a public or political figure.
Any ruthless killer.
To assassinate.
The state or quality of being unkind.
An unkind act.
The collective noun for ravens
Having no brain.
Unintelligent, with little common sense.
To watch or tend someone else's child for a period of time, often for money.
definitions.example: My daughter is babysitting for the Morgans at number ten, who are going out on a date night.

definitions.definition: To watch or attend anything or anyone unnecessarily closely; to have to help or coax too much.
definitions.example: He left me to babysit the new guy while he got some work done.

definitions.definition: Without love.
definitions.example: Theirs was a loveless marriage: they stayed together even though they didn't care for each other.

definitions.definition: A vector quantity that denotes the rate of change of position with respect to time, or a speed with the directional component.
Rapidity of motion.
The rate of occurrence.
The number of times that an average unit of currency is spent during a specific period of time.
So as to form a flat surface.
definitions.example: Spread the icing evenly over the cake.

definitions.definition: In a fair manner of distribution, giving the same amount or number to each; equally.
definitions.example: To avoid arguments, he divided the sweets evenly between his two children.

definitions.definition: In a manner that leaves no remainder.
definitions.example: 12 is evenly divisible by 2, 3, 4 and 6.

definitions.definition: In terms of or by means of even numbers.
